name: Access Auditor & OpenAPI Integration
description: Walks OpenAPI response schemas at adapter slot time, maps each field against the classification registry using fnmatch/regex patterns, and produces a structural access profile (superset of data tiers the node can return). Compares structural profile against the node's declared data_access from the access graph. Flags DECLARATION_GAP if structural profile includes undeclared tiers (C005). Produces BLOCK_SLOT if undeclared tiers are present and block_on_gate is configured. Records access violation findings when observed output tier exceeds declared reads (FA-A-015). Handles missing OpenAPI schemas gracefully by recording an INCOMPLETE_SCHEMA warning (rabbit hole patch). Composed of four concerns: schema_walker (pure DFS traversal), classifier (pure field→tier mapping), auditor orchestrator (composition + gating), and shared models/types.

types.name: DataTier
types.description: IntEnum representing data classification tiers. Integer value determines ordering/severity. PUBLIC(0) < INTERNAL(1) < CONFIDENTIAL(2) < RESTRICTED(3). Used for natural ordering comparisons in access auditing.

types.name: FindingCode
types.description: StrEnum of well-known finding codes emitted by the access auditor. Stable string identifiers used for gating decisions and ledger records.

types.name: FindingSeverity
types.description: IntEnum representing finding severity levels. Integer value determines ordering. INFO(0) < WARNING(1) < ERROR(2) < CRITICAL(3).

types.name: SlotDecisionVerdict
types.description: Literal verdict for a slot gating decision. ALLOW permits the slot; BLOCK prevents it.

types.name: NodeId
types.description: NewType over str. Identifies a unique node in the access graph. Must not be empty. Prevents accidental mixing with other string-typed identifiers under strict mypy.

types.name: FieldPath
types.description: NewType over str. Dot-separated JSON path to a leaf field in an OpenAPI response schema (e.g. 'response.user.email'). Must not be empty.

types.name: AdapterSlotId
types.description: NewType over str. Identifies a unique adapter slot being audited. Must not be empty.

types.name: FieldEntry

fields.name: path
fields.description: Dot-separated path from schema root to this leaf field.

fields.name: field_type
fields.description: OpenAPI type string for this field (e.g. 'string', 'integer', 'boolean', 'array').

fields.name: nullable
fields.description: Whether the field is marked nullable in the schema.

fields.name: format_hint
fields.description: Optional OpenAPI format hint (e.g. 'email', 'date-time', 'uuid'). Empty string if not specified.

types.description: A single leaf field discovered by the schema walker. Represents one terminal node in the DFS traversal of an OpenAPI response schema.

types.name: SchemaWarning

fields.name: code
fields.description: Always INCOMPLETE_SCHEMA for schema walker warnings.

fields.name: path
fields.description: The JSON path or $ref URI where the issue was encountered. Empty string if schema was entirely missing.

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable description including the specific location and nature of the incompleteness.

types.description: A warning generated during schema walking when the schema is incomplete, missing, or contains unresolvable references.

types.name: WalkResult

fields.name: fields
fields.description: All leaf FieldEntry instances discovered by DFS traversal.

fields.name: warnings
fields.description: Schema warnings encountered during traversal (missing schemas, unresolvable $refs, cycles).

types.description: Result of walking an OpenAPI response schema. Contains discovered leaf fields and any warnings about schema incompleteness.

types.name: ClassifiedField

fields.name: path
fields.description: The field path that was classified.

fields.name: tier
fields.description: The data classification tier assigned to this field.

fields.name: matched_pattern
fields.description: The fnmatch/regex pattern from the registry that matched this field. Empty string if defaulted to PUBLIC.

types.description: A field entry paired with its resolved data classification tier from the registry.

types.name: ClassificationResult

fields.name: classified_fields
fields.description: All fields with their assigned data tier.

fields.name: tier_set
fields.description: Deduplicated set of all DataTier values present in classified_fields. Serialized as sorted list; in-memory representation is frozenset[DataTier].

types.description: Result of classifying a list of field entries against the classification registry.

types.name: StructuralProfile

fields.name: node_id
fields.description: The node whose schema was analyzed.

fields.name: adapter_slot_id
fields.description: The adapter slot at which the schema was inspected.

fields.name: endpoint
fields.description: The OpenAPI endpoint path that was analyzed (e.g. '/api/v1/users').

fields.name: tiers
fields.description: All data tiers present in the response schema. In-memory: frozenset[DataTier]. Serialized as sorted list of tier names.

fields.name: classified_fields
fields.description: Complete mapping of each discovered field path to its classification tier.

fields.name: warnings
fields.description: Any schema warnings encountered during profile computation.

fields.name: schema_complete
fields.description: True if the schema was fully resolved with no warnings. False triggers assume-worst behavior.

fields.name: computed_at
fields.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when this profile was computed.

types.description: The structural access profile for a node at a given endpoint. Represents the superset of data tiers the node can return based on its OpenAPI response schema. Pydantic model with deterministic serialization.

types.name: AccessFinding

fields.name: code
fields.description: The finding code (C005, FA_A_015, INCOMPLETE_SCHEMA).

fields.name: severity
fields.description: The severity level of this finding.

fields.name: node_id
fields.description: The node that triggered this finding.

fields.name: adapter_slot_id
fields.description: The adapter slot context for this finding.

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable description. Must include specific node_id, field path, and/or domain that caused the error per operating procedures.

validators.error_message: Finding message must be descriptive (at least 10 characters).

fields.name: evidence
fields.description: Structured evidence supporting this finding.

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when this finding was produced.

types.description: A finding produced by the access auditor. Immutable record of a detected access violation or gap. All fields required for ledger append. Message always includes the specific node_id, field, and domain.

types.name: AccessFindingEvidence

fields.name: structural_tiers
fields.description: The tiers found in the structural profile.

fields.name: declared_tiers
fields.description: The tiers declared by the node in the access graph.

fields.name: undeclared_tiers
fields.description: The set difference: structural_tiers - declared_tiers.

fields.name: fields_by_undeclared_tier
fields.description: Mapping of undeclared tier name → list of field paths at that tier. JSON-serializable dict[str, list[str]].

fields.name: endpoint
fields.description: The endpoint being audited.

types.description: Structured evidence attached to an AccessFinding. Contains the data needed to reproduce and understand the finding.

types.name: GateConfig

fields.name: block_on_codes
fields.description: Set of FindingCode values that trigger BLOCK_SLOT. If any finding's code is in this set, the slot is blocked. In-memory: set[FindingCode]. Serialized as sorted list.

fields.name: assume_worst_on_incomplete
fields.description: If true (default), missing/incomplete schemas cause the profile to include RESTRICTED tier.

types.description: Configuration for slot gating decisions. Determines which finding codes trigger a BLOCK verdict.

types.name: SlotDecision

fields.name: decision
fields.description: ALLOW if no blocking findings; BLOCK if any finding code is in GateConfig.block_on_codes.

fields.name: adapter_slot_id
fields.description: The adapter slot this decision applies to.

fields.name: node_id
fields.description: The node this decision applies to.

fields.name: findings
fields.description: All findings produced during slot auditing. May be empty for ALLOW with no issues.

fields.name: blocking_codes
fields.description: The subset of finding codes that triggered the BLOCK decision. Empty if ALLOW.

fields.name: profile
fields.description: The structural profile computed for this slot.

fields.name: decided_at
fields.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when the decision was made.

types.description: The gating decision for an adapter slot. Contains the verdict (ALLOW or BLOCK) and all findings that contributed to the decision.

types.name: DeclaredAccess

fields.name: node_id
fields.description: The node making the declaration.

fields.name: declared_read_tiers
fields.description: The data tiers the node declares it reads. In-memory: frozenset[DataTier].

fields.name: declared_write_tiers
fields.description: The data tiers the node declares it writes. In-memory: frozenset[DataTier].

types.description: The declared data access for a node as extracted from the access graph. Represents the node's authority claim about what tiers it accesses.

types.name: ClassificationRegistryEntry

fields.name: field_pattern
fields.description: An fnmatch glob or regex pattern to match against field paths.

validators.error_message: Field pattern must not be empty.

fields.name: tier
fields.description: The data tier to assign to fields matching this pattern.

fields.name: pattern_type
fields.description: Either 'fnmatch' or 'regex'. Determines matching strategy.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Human-readable description of what this pattern classifies.

types.description: A single entry in the classification registry mapping a field pattern to a data tier. Used by ClassificationRegistry.match().

types.name: ObservedOutput

fields.name: node_id
fields.description: The node that produced the output.

fields.name: adapter_slot_id
fields.description: The adapter slot where the output was observed.

fields.name: observed_tiers
fields.description: Data tiers actually present in the observed output. In-memory: frozenset[DataTier].

fields.name: observed_fields
fields.description: The classified fields from the observed output.

fields.name: observed_at
fields.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of observation.

types.description: Runtime observation of actual data tiers present in a node's output. Used for FA-A-015 runtime violation detection.

types.name: RefResolver
types.description: Protocol/Callable type: (ref_uri: str) → dict. Resolves a $ref URI to the referenced schema dict. Implementations may read from file or in-memory cache.

functions.name: walk_response_schema
functions.description: Pure function. DFS-traverses an OpenAPI response schema, resolves $ref references via the provided resolver with cycle detection (visited set), flattens allOf/anyOf/oneOf with union semantics, and emits a FieldEntry for every leaf field. Returns INCOMPLETE_SCHEMA warnings for missing/empty schemas or unresolvable $refs. Does not perform classification — only structural discovery.

inputs.name: schema
inputs.description: The OpenAPI response schema object to walk. May be empty or None-equivalent (triggers INCOMPLETE_SCHEMA warning).

inputs.name: ref_resolver
inputs.description: Callable that resolves $ref URIs to schema dicts. Signature: (ref_uri: str) → dict.

inputs.name: root_path
inputs.description: The prefix path for all discovered fields. Defaults to 'response'.

inputs.name: max_depth
inputs.description: Maximum DFS traversal depth to prevent stack overflow on deep schemas. Defaults to 64.

error_cases.name: invalid_schema_type
error_cases.condition: schema is not a dict (after None-check) and not a recognized schema type

error_data.detail: schema must be a dict or None, got {type}

error_cases.name: ref_resolver_failure
error_cases.condition: ref_resolver raises an exception for a $ref URI

error_cases.name: max_depth_exceeded
error_cases.condition: Traversal depth exceeds max_depth without reaching a leaf

preconditions: ref_resolver is a callable accepting a single str argument and returning dict
postconditions: Every FieldEntry in result.fields has a non-empty path starting with root_path
If schema is empty or None-equivalent, result.warnings contains at least one INCOMPLETE_SCHEMA warning
No circular $ref chains cause infinite recursion — cycles are detected and emitted as INCOMPLETE_SCHEMA warnings
result.fields contains only leaf fields (no intermediate object/array nodes)
Function is pure: same inputs always produce same outputs

structured_side_effects.description: Pure function with no side effects.

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is the total number of nodes in the schema tree

functions.name: classify_fields
functions.description: Pure function. Takes a list of FieldEntry and a ClassificationRegistry, matches each field path against the registry's fnmatch/regex patterns, and returns a ClassificationResult with each field mapped to a DataTier. Unmatched fields default to PUBLIC. The tier_set is the frozenset of all unique tiers found.

inputs.name: fields
inputs.description: List of FieldEntry instances to classify. May be empty.

inputs.name: registry_entries
inputs.description: List of ClassificationRegistryEntry defining the patterns and their tiers. Evaluated in order; first match wins (highest tier wins on tie).

error_cases.name: invalid_regex_pattern
error_cases.condition: A registry entry with pattern_type='regex' has an invalid regex expression

error_cases.name: empty_field_path
error_cases.condition: A FieldEntry has an empty path string

preconditions: All FieldEntry in fields have non-empty path
All ClassificationRegistryEntry in registry_entries have valid pattern_type ('fnmatch' or 'regex')
Regex patterns in registry_entries are valid and compilable
postconditions: len(result.classified_fields) == len(fields)
Every field in fields appears exactly once in result.classified_fields
result.tier_set == frozenset(cf.tier for cf in result.classified_fields)
Fields matching no pattern are assigned DataTier.PUBLIC
If a field matches multiple patterns, the pattern yielding the highest DataTier wins
Function is pure: same inputs always produce same outputs

structured_side_effects.description: Pure function with no side effects.

performance_budget.complexity: O(f * p) where f is number of fields and p is number of registry patterns

functions.name: compute_structural_profile
functions.description: Orchestrator function. Composes walk_response_schema and classify_fields to produce a complete StructuralProfile for a node at a given adapter slot. If the schema is missing or incomplete and GateConfig.assume_worst_on_incomplete is true, RESTRICTED tier is injected into the profile. Records the profile computation timestamp in UTC.

inputs.name: node_id
inputs.description: The node being profiled.

inputs.name: adapter_slot_id
inputs.description: The adapter slot being audited.

inputs.name: endpoint
inputs.description: The OpenAPI endpoint path being analyzed.

inputs.name: schema
inputs.description: The OpenAPI response schema dict. May be empty for missing schemas.

inputs.name: ref_resolver
inputs.description: Callable for resolving $ref URIs.

inputs.name: registry_entries
inputs.description: Classification registry entries for field → tier mapping.

inputs.name: gate_config
inputs.description: Gate configuration controlling assume-worst behavior.

postconditions: result.node_id == node_id
result.adapter_slot_id == adapter_slot_id
result.endpoint == endpoint
result.computed_at is a valid ISO 8601 UTC timestamp
If schema is empty and gate_config.assume_worst_on_incomplete is True, DataTier.RESTRICTED is in result.tiers
result.schema_complete == (len(result.warnings) == 0)
result.classified_fields is consistent with result.tiers

structured_side_effects.description: Pure orchestration of pure functions. No side effects.

performance_budget.complexity: O(n + f*p) where n=schema nodes, f=fields, p=patterns

functions.name: audit_slot
functions.description: Core auditing function. Compares a structural profile's tiers against the node's declared access from the access graph. Produces DECLARATION_GAP (C005) findings for any tier in the structural profile not present in declared access. Returns a SlotDecision with BLOCK verdict if any C005 finding code is in gate_config.block_on_codes. Also includes INCOMPLETE_SCHEMA findings from the profile warnings. Appends all findings to the ledger via LedgerWriter.

inputs.name: profile
inputs.description: The computed structural profile for the node/slot.

inputs.name: declared_access
inputs.description: The node's declared data access from the access graph.

inputs.name: gate_config
inputs.description: Gate configuration controlling which findings trigger BLOCK.

error_data.detail: node_id mismatch between profile and declared access

preconditions: profile is a valid StructuralProfile with computed_at set
postconditions: result.node_id == profile.node_id
result.adapter_slot_id == profile.adapter_slot_id
result.profile == profile
result.decision == BLOCK if and only if any finding.code is in gate_config.block_on_codes
result.decision == ALLOW if result.blocking_codes is empty
For each tier in (profile.tiers - declared_access.declared_read_tiers), exactly one C005 finding exists in result.findings
All findings have severity >= WARNING for C005, >= INFO for INCOMPLETE_SCHEMA
Every finding message includes the node_id and specific undeclared tier(s)
result.decided_at is a valid ISO 8601 UTC timestamp
All findings are appended to the ledger via LedgerWriter before return

structured_side_effects.description: Appends AccessFinding records to the append-only trust ledger via LedgerWriter protocol.
Logs slot decision at INFO level; logs BLOCK decisions at WARNING level.

performance_budget.complexity: O(t + f) where t=number of tiers, f=number of classified fields for evidence construction

functions.name: audit_observed_output
functions.description: Runtime observation auditor. Compares actually observed data tiers in a node's output against the node's declared read tiers. Produces FA-A-015 findings when observed tiers exceed declared reads. This catches runtime violations that structural analysis alone cannot detect. Appends findings to the ledger via LedgerWriter.

inputs.name: observed
inputs.description: The observed output with classified fields and tiers.

inputs.name: declared_access
inputs.description: The node's declared data access from the access graph.

error_data.detail: node_id mismatch between observed output and declared access

preconditions: observed.observed_at is a valid ISO 8601 UTC timestamp
postconditions: For each tier in (observed.observed_tiers - declared_access.declared_read_tiers), exactly one FA_A_015 finding exists in the result
If observed.observed_tiers is a subset of declared_access.declared_read_tiers, result is an empty list
Every finding has code == FA_A_015
Every finding has severity >= ERROR
Every finding message includes the node_id, the specific undeclared tier, and the field paths at that tier
All findings are appended to the ledger via LedgerWriter before return
Each finding timestamp is >= observed.observed_at

structured_side_effects.description: Appends FA-A-015 AccessFinding records to the append-only trust ledger via LedgerWriter protocol.
Logs observed access violations at ERROR level.

performance_budget.complexity: O(t + f) where t=observed tiers, f=observed fields

functions.name: load_gate_config
functions.description: Loads and validates a GateConfig from a YAML configuration file or dict. Provides sensible defaults: block_on_codes=[C005], assume_worst_on_incomplete=true.

inputs.name: config_source
inputs.description: Raw configuration dict (typically loaded from YAML). Keys: 'block_on_codes' (list of FindingCode strings), 'assume_worst_on_incomplete' (bool).

error_cases.name: invalid_finding_code
error_cases.condition: A value in config_source['block_on_codes'] is not a valid FindingCode

postconditions: result is a valid GateConfig
If 'block_on_codes' is missing from config_source, result.block_on_codes defaults to [C005]
If 'assume_worst_on_incomplete' is missing, result.assume_worst_on_incomplete defaults to True
All values in result.block_on_codes are valid FindingCode variants

structured_side_effects.description: Pure validation and construction of GateConfig.

functions.name: load_classification_registry
functions.description: Loads and validates classification registry entries from a YAML file or dict list. Pre-compiles regex patterns for performance. Validates all patterns are syntactically correct and all tiers are valid DataTier values.

inputs.name: registry_source
inputs.description: List of raw dicts, each with keys: 'field_pattern' (str), 'tier' (str matching DataTier name), 'pattern_type' ('fnmatch' or 'regex'), optional 'description' (str).

error_cases.name: invalid_tier
error_cases.condition: A registry entry has a tier value that is not a valid DataTier name

error_cases.name: invalid_regex
error_cases.condition: A registry entry with pattern_type='regex' has an un-compilable regex

error_cases.name: missing_required_key
error_cases.condition: A registry entry dict is missing 'field_pattern', 'tier', or 'pattern_type'

error_cases.name: invalid_pattern_type
error_cases.condition: A registry entry has pattern_type not in ('fnmatch', 'regex')

preconditions: registry_source is a list of dicts
Each dict in registry_source has at least 'field_pattern', 'tier', and 'pattern_type' keys
postconditions: len(result) == len(registry_source)
All regex patterns in result have been validated as compilable
All tier values in result are valid DataTier variants
All pattern_type values are 'fnmatch' or 'regex'

structured_side_effects.description: Pure validation and construction of ClassificationRegistryEntry list.

invariants: DataTier ordering is PUBLIC(0) < INTERNAL(1) < CONFIDENTIAL(2) < RESTRICTED(3); integer comparison determines severity
The trust ledger is append-only — no updates, no deletes; all findings appended via LedgerWriter protocol
Trust and authority are distinct: structural profiles are computed (earned), declared_access is declared (authority)
Structural profiles use frozenset[DataTier] — immutable and hashable, enabling caching per schema version
Missing or empty OpenAPI schemas are treated as RESTRICTED tier (assume-worst fail-safe)
All findings carry the specific node_id, field path, and domain that caused the error in the message field
Deterministic serialization: Pydantic model_dump(mode='json') with sorted keys for all ledger entries
Classification of unmatched fields defaults to PUBLIC (DataTier.PUBLIC = 0)
Schema walker cycle detection prevents infinite recursion on circular $ref chains
FindingCode values are stable string identifiers: C005 for DECLARATION_GAP, FA_A_015 for observed access violation
Pure functions (walk_response_schema, classify_fields) have no side effects and are deterministic given same input
SlotDecision.decision is BLOCK if and only if at least one finding code is in GateConfig.block_on_codes
Protocol-based interfaces (ClassificationRegistry, LedgerWriter, AccessGraph) use structural subtyping — no inheritance required
